title: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tag Attributes

statement.markdown: Given a string representing an HTML anchor tag, validate its attributes and return the corrected tag if any attribute is missing or invalid. The corrected tag should have the href attribute, target attribute, and a valid text content. If the input is already a valid anchor tag, return the original string.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing an HTML anchor tag, e.g., 'Example Link'
statement.outputFormat: A string representing the corrected HTML anchor tag, or the original input if it is already valid
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), where n is the length of the input string. Space complexity: O(n), for the output string.

examples.explanation: The target attribute is missing, so it is added.
The href attribute is missing, so it is added with a default value.
The target attribute is set to _self, which is not valid, so it is changed to _blank.

hints.text: Check the existence and validity of the href and target attributes.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tag Attributes
editorial.intuition: The input string should be parsed to extract the tag and its attributes. Then, check the existence and validity of the href and target attributes. If any attribute is missing or invalid, add or correct it accordingly.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the tag and its attributes to find the missing or invalid ones. Then, add or correct them.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to extract the tag and its attributes. Then, use a dictionary to check the existence and validity of the attributes.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to extract the tag and its attributes. Then, use a dictionary to check the existence and validity of the attributes. If any attribute is missing or invalid, add or correct it accordingly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected tag should have the href attribute, target attribute, and a valid text content. If the input is already a valid anchor tag, return the original string.
editorial.dryRun: Input: 'Example Link'. Output: 'Example Link'.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add the target attribute

solutions.javascript: // Your code here return 'Example Link';
